content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Evaluated amendment fr-7-aa318c37 proposing to use tokio::time::pause() or start_paused=true in the spawn_reap_with_timeout_drops_on_expiry test. The test currently relies on wall-clock timing (50ms timeout + 300ms sleep) which is a well-known source of CI flakiness. The fix is straightforward and correct since both the production code (tokio::time::timeout) and the test (tokio::time::sleep) use tokio timers that work deterministically under paused time.

## Votes

- `fr-7-aa318c37`: Accept
  This is a legitimate robustness issue. The test uses wall-clock timing: a 50ms timeout inside spawn_reap_with_timeout (via tokio::time::timeout) and a 300ms tokio::time::sleep to wait for it. Under CI load, tokio task scheduling delays can cause the spawned task to not even start before the 300ms sleep completes in the test, or worse, the timeout may not fire accurately. Using #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)] is the idiomatic fix — both tokio::time::timeout and tokio::time::sleep advance deterministically under simulated time, with auto-advance ensuring the 50ms timeout fires before the 300ms sleep completes. The inner future uses std::future::pending() (not a real I/O operation), so paused time is fully compatible. This eliminates a real flakiness vector with a one-line annotation change.
